back_translation: 'How you have helped the powerless and saved the feeble arm!
How you have counseled the unwise and made known sound insight so fully!
To whom have you spoken these words, and whose spirit spoke through you?
The dead tremble, those beneath the waters and those who dwell in them.
Sheol is naked before God, and Abaddon has no covering.
He stretches out the northern sky over empty space and hangs the earth upon nothing.
He wraps up the waters in His clouds, yet the clouds do not burst under their weight.
He covers the face of the full moon, spreading His cloud over it.
He has inscribed a horizon on the surface of the waters, at the boundary between light and darkness.
The pillars of heaven quake and are astounded at His rebuke.
By His power He stirred up the sea, and by His understanding He shattered Rahab.
By His breath the skies became clear; His hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
Behold, these are but the fringes of His ways; how faint is the whisper of Him that we hear! Who then can understand the thunder of His power?'
